growth_records.cite: Laursen MF et al. Bifidobacterium species associated with breastfeeding produce aromatic lactic acids in the infant gut. Nature Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41564-021-00970-4. [PMC8556157]
growth_records.snippet: The cultures were sampled (500 ul) every hour for OD600nm measurements and the culture supernatants ... from early (13 h) stationary phase were analysed by UPLC-MS for aromatic amino metabolites and by GC-MS for lactate. ... Curves show mea
growth_records.notes: This is NOT a genome-scale-model / 13C-MFA paper; it is a Bifidobacterium physiology/enzymology study. The only extractable-in-text quantitative metabolic data are in-vitro ENZYME kinetics for the type-4 LDH/ALDH (kcat/K0.5: indolepyruvate 194 s-1 mM-1, 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate 16 s-1 mM-1, phenylpyruvate 11 s-1 mM-1; Fig. 3) - these are purified-enzyme parameters, NOT whole-cell growth-condition uptake/secretion fluxes, so not entered as rates. The whole-cell growth physiology that IS reported (specific growth / doubling times in Fig. 2d; secreted ILA, PLA, 4-OH-PLA and lactate amounts in Fig. 2e; production by ~14 type strains on glucose vs HMO in Fig. 2a) has its NUMERIC values only in figure images / Source data files, which are not present in the PMC XML fulltext. Therefore all rate fields are null (no numbers invented, per instruction). One representative record emitted for the genetically defined B. longum ssp. longum 105-A WT batch condition to preserve organism+medium+condition and the qualitative measured secretion. Medium = MRSc = MRS broth + 2% (w/v) glucose + 0.05% (w/v) L-cysteine (base MRS component list not itemised in paper). Conditions: 37 C, anaerobic, batch, shaking; supernatant at 13 h early stationary phase. No pH, no growth rate value, no yields extractable from text. Growth was not impaired by type-4 ldh disruption (Fig. 2d).

growth_records.agit: 300 rpm (stir bar) for monoculture growth curves; Hungate tubes otherwise
growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]

growth_records.notes: μ=0.91 h^-1 (±0.04) reported. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.91 = 0.762 h. Max OD600 = 1.01. Only Actinobacteria member that grew.
